topics.label: Process Scheduling & Context Switching
topics.description: The mechanisms the OS uses to manage process execution transitions and resource allocation among competing processes.

topics.label: Concurrency, Synchronization & Deadlocks
topics.description: The application of primitives like semaphores and mutexes to manage shared resources and the logic required to prevent or resolve deadlock conditions.

topics.label: Virtual Memory & Paging Mechanisms
topics.description: The hardware and software processes involved in address translation and the mapping of virtual memory spaces to physical memory frames.

topics.label: Page Replacement & Demand Paging
topics.description: The logic used to manage memory pressure through page fault handling and the implementation of replacement algorithms like LRU or FIFO.

topics.label: File System Architecture & I/O Management
topics.description: How the operating system organizes persistent data on storage media and manages hardware-level I/O requests and disk scheduling.
